question_text: Three forces act on a box: a push of 80 N to the right, a push of 50 N to the left, and friction of 30 N opposing the motion. Calculate the resultant force on the box and explain what will happen to the box's motion.
correct_answer: Resultant force = 0 N. The box will move at constant velocity (or remain at rest if already stationary) because the forces are balanced.
explanation: First, find the net push: 80 N (right) - 50 N (left) = 30 N to the right. Then subtract friction opposing this motion: 30 N - 30 N = 0 N resultant force. When the resultant force is zero, the object either stays at rest or continues moving at constant velocity (Newton's first law).
